key_requirements: Existing Phase 1+2 check() and check_with_coverage() entry points in spec_alignment::check module
CheckResult schema: { files, total_violations, passed, coverage } — Phase 3 consumers need violations as alignment_warnings
ViolationKind enum covers all violation types that need to be surfaced in workflow responses
Scope boundary in Phase 2 spec explicitly defers workflow integration to Phase 3 (#1142)
Architecture: spec_alignment::check(path) is callable from any tool — takes a Path, returns CheckResult synchronously
sdd_run_change result schema currently has: change_id, action, current_phase, executor, message, next_actions — needs alignment_warnings added
Q3 answer: alignment_warnings field must carry full violation list as complete JSON in sdd_run_change response
sdd_workflow_create_change_merge response schema: currently has specs_merged, archive_path, audit_log, git_commit_sha, pr_url, git_warning — may need alignment_warnings too
workflow/mod.rs route() function returns Value — alignment check runs after routing and injects warnings field
Q1 answer: sdd_artifact_create_change_spec must run spec_alignment::check() on the ENTIRE spec file after writing any section — not just the section written
Artifact tool response currently returns filled_sections, status, next_actions — needs alignment_violations array added
payload_path convention: CLI invocation pattern cclab sdd artifact create-change-spec {change_id} {spec_id} — check runs on the spec file path after write
Artifact tool is the last step that writes to the spec file before review — validation at this point catches format/logical errors early
Q2 answer: after specs are merged to cclab/specs/, run check() on each merged spec and append alignment warnings to implementation.md review section
Merge flow: FindSpecs → ReadFM → Strip → Write → Archive → Move → GitOps — alignment check inserts between Write and Archive steps
implementation.md format: inline ## Review: {spec_id} sections — alignment warnings appended there as ## Alignment Warnings subsection
Non-fatal: alignment violations should NOT block the merge — they are warnings only (consistent with Q2: 'write to implementation.md')
Existing merge writes to cclab/specs/{main_spec_ref} — check runs on that written path
Phase routing table: ChangeSpec* phases → sdd_workflow_create_change_spec; ChangeMerge* → sdd_workflow_create_change_merge
sdd_run_change is the outer bridge — alignment_warnings added to its response means the skill sees them on every loop iteration
State transitions do not change for Phase 3 — alignment check is a non-blocking side effect, not a new state
Section fill lifecycle: skeleton → fill sections via sdd_artifact_create_change_spec → prune → review — Q1 check runs after each fill call
Artifact writing enforcement: agents must use CLI artifact tools — the post-write check runs inside the tool, not the CLI wrapper
filled_sections tracking in frontmatter: check runs on the full spec file, not just the section just written
create_complete=true: spec is fully filled — this is not when check runs (check runs on every section write)
implementation.md inline review format: ## Review: {spec_id} sections with Verdict, Summary, Checklist
Q2: alignment warnings appended to the review section of implementation.md — need to know the exact format to append correctly
implementation.md is created by sdd_artifact_create_change_implementation — it already exists when merge runs, so append is safe
Revision tracking in STATE.yaml: task_revisions field — alignment warnings are informational, do not affect revision count
Skill loop calls sdd_run_change repeatedly — alignment_warnings in response will be visible to mainthread on each iteration
Q3 implies skill should surface alignment_warnings to user when present (display or log)
Executor dispatch branches: mainthread, subagent:*, other — alignment_warnings is a passive field, no new branch needed
Verification step after agent: skill checks STATE.yaml phase + artifact existence — alignment_warnings does not affect verification logic
Artifact writing enforcement pattern (two-layer: prompt constraint + post-agent verification) is reused by change-spec phase — context for understanding where artifact tools are called
Agent retry mechanism: MAX_AGENT_RETRIES=2 — alignment check result is returned in tool response, retry logic is unchanged
